evidence.work_arrangement: This is a hybrid role with a minimum of 1 day per week in our Cambridge office.
evidence.velocity_vs_rigour: Is genuinely comfortable with ambiguity, you'd rather ask "what decision is this for?" than start writing SQL immediately.
evidence.velocity_vs_rigour_explanation: The JD balances a speed/pragmatism register (fast, ad hoc answers to business questions) with rigour signals (sanity-check your own findings, be honest about uncertainty), landing on mixed rather than either pole.
evidence.domain_risk: whether it feeds into product, commercial, or engineering decisions
evidence.domain_risk_explanation: CMR Surgical is a medical device company, but the analytics role itself is framed around general business-decision support (usage tracking, commercial/product questions) without regulatory/compliance stakes for the role, hence moderate rather than high.
evidence.data_team_maturity: You'll work in a highly supportive, cross-functional team of subject matter experts
evidence.data_team_maturity_explanation: The JD describes an existing, established data team with subject matter experts and a defined remit, actively growing rather than being built from scratch, indicating mid maturity.
evidence.jd_authorship: This isn't a build-the-perfect-pipeline role, it's a figure-out-what's-actually-going-on role.
evidence.jd_authorship_explanation: The JD mixes vivid, specific hiring-manager framing of the actual day-to-day work (concrete example scenarios) with more generic boilerplate about values and inclusivity, landing on mixed.
evidence.stakeholder_orientation: working directly with stakeholders across the business who come to you with real, unresolved questions
evidence.stakeholder_orientation_explanation: Stakeholders are described generically as "across the business" spanning product, commercial, and engineering with no single group dominant, so mixed applies.
evidence.autonomy_level: Take ambiguous, open-ended business questions from stakeholders across the business, and turn them into clear, well-evidenced answers.
evidence.autonomy_level_explanation: The candidate responds to questions stakeholders bring rather than setting the data team's own direction or priorities, an execution-oriented framing despite high analytical judgment.
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix: You'll work in a highly supportive, cross-functional team of subject matter experts
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix_explanation: The role joins an existing data team and works with existing (if messy) datasets rather than building new infrastructure from scratch.
evidence.ai_role: Is comfortable using AI-assisted analysis tools to work faster
evidence.ai_role_explanation: The candidate is expected to use AI-assisted tools to accelerate their own analysis work, a clear ai_user signal.
evidence.testing_framing: No testing or data quality signal.
evidence.testing_framing_explanation: The role is analysis- and insight-focused (ambiguous business questions, exploratory analysis) with no mention of testing, data contracts, or data quality ownership.
evidence.loss_aversion_framing_explanation: The JD is framed entirely in terms of exploration, ambiguity, and decision-enablement with no risk, compliance, or operational-reliability register.
